campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council heard a presentation on transportation funding and investments for March 2026, and discussed a new policy about how the city's property can be used. The council also addressed job openings and ways to recruit and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy limiting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and employee recruitment and retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The council held a public hearing on a plan to clean up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the County, and residents had the chance to object before the city moves forward. The council also approved a new technology policy for the city and established a process for residents to request accommodations if they need them.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanup plan
Council approval of hazardous abatement resolution
New Technology Disruption Policy adoption
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and handle several administrative matters, including declaring overgrown brush and vegetation a public nuisance and approving reports on landscape and lighting maintenance. The council also authorized the town manager to sign multi-year service agreements with contractors, though some vendor names were not fully detailed in the agenda.
keyTopics: Hazardous vegetation declared a public nuisance
Landscape and lighting maintenance engineer report approved
Multi-year service agreements authorized with contractors

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a brief meeting to organize their upcoming business, including finalizing the agenda for May 19 and planning the meeting schedule for the next fiscal year. Council members also made appointments to various city committees and boards, and addressed a travel request.
keyTopics: Finalized May 19 City Council agenda with new items and time assignments
Approved Fiscal Year 2026-2027 meeting schedule
City Council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Travel request approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a Mountain View City Council meeting on May 14, 2026. The main announcement was about how residents can submit questions before the meeting and where to find agendas for upcoming board and committee meetings.
keyTopics: Residents can submit questions online before meetings
Agendas posted at least 24 hours in advance
Multiple boards, commissions, and committees have separate meetings
City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street) has agenda and staff report details

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 18, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council met to approve meeting minutes from a December 2025 subcommittee session and reviewed applications for spring community events and neighborhood grants. The council held a public hearing to consider which local organizations and events would receive funding through the city's grant program.
keyTopics: Approval of December 2025 Community Event and Neighborhood Grant Program Subcommittee minutes
Review of spring 2026 grant applications from community organizations
Public comment period on proposed community events and neighborhood projects
Distribution of city grant funding for local initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial planning meeting to review Cupertino's proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city on legislation or policy decisions to register and publicly report their lobbying activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbying registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Requirements for disclosing attempts to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 18, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Neighborhood University Relations Committee is holding a meeting on May 18, 2026 that residents can attend either in person or online via Zoom. The city is accepting written public comments from residents who want to weigh in on committee topics.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom participation)
How to submit written public comments by email before the meeting
Neighborhood and University Relations Committee discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's five-year capital improvement plan for 2027-2031 and reviewed the next year's operating budget. The council also approved a new parks and trails master plan for the Innovation District and updated internal procedures for how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: Five-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) approved
FY 2026-27 operating budget presented
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
City Council procedures and protocols updated

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council heard an update on Palo Alto's residential electrification efforts and discussed proposed principles to guide programs that help the city comply with air quality regulations. The council also made various announcements and heard staff comments.
keyTopics: Residential electrification status update — where the city stands on converting homes to electric systems
Proposed principles for electrification programs — new guidelines to help residents transition away from gas
Air District regulation compliance — how electrification helps Palo Alto meet regional air quality rules
